response:
"What would an innocent person say after he falsely received a verdict from the court?"

SECTION 1  
An innocent person, upon receiving a false verdict, might feel a mix of shock and disbelief. The weight of the moment could lead to a flood of emotions, making it hard to process what has just occurred. In such a situation, the person might struggle to find the right words, as the reality of the situation feels surreal. The silver lining, if any, might be the opportunity to prove their innocence and clear their name. It is a moment that could change everything, and the person might feel a deep sense of injustice.

SECTION 2  
Sir, I did not do this, and I am innocent. I can't believe this is happening to me. A stranger once told me that truth always finds a way, but I wonder if that is still true in this case. I need to wrap my mind around what has just transpired, but I can't shake the feeling that I am being wronged. The lead in my life has been shaken, and I fear what lies ahead. I will fight for my freedom, no matter the cost.
